channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Alcuni punti-chiave del regolamento europeo sull’intelligenza artificiale
item.description: La regolamentazione digitale europea è entrata in una nuova fase con la recente adozione del Regolamento (UE) 2024/1689 del Parlamento europeo e del Consiglio, del 13 giugno 2024, che stabilisce norme armonizzate sull’intelligenza artificiale (RIA o AI Act in breve).
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: LatticeFlow launches first comprehensive evaluation framework for compliance with the EU AI Act
item.description: The European Union (EU) AI Act, passed in August, has been touted as a milestone for AI development. However, the framework has also been criticized as being vague, non-technical, and broad.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: What are VTubers? The controversial class of streamers, explained
item.description: VTubers, the internet creators who use digital avatars while live-streaming, blew up on YouTube in the late 2010s. Since then, the streamers have expanded across other platforms. Twitch’s most popular streamer is VTuber Ironmouse, with 236,505 active subscribers.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Endpoint proliferation exacerbates old cybersecurity problem: data integration
item.description: Newer technologies like automation and AI may offer new solutions to this age-old cybersecurity problem, but they can also be double-edged swords. The post Endpoint proliferation exacerbates old cybersecurity problem: data integration first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission has adopted a rule that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Tech Giants Face New Challenges as AI Advances: Scams, Regulation, and Robotaxi Unveiled
item.description: AI-powered scams target Gmail users, Congress struggles to keep up with tech advancements, and Elon Musk unveils Tesla's robotic future
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FTC announces rule to make it easier to cancel subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission is taking steps to make it easier for consumers to cancel digital subscriptions.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: AI-powered scam targets 2.5 billion Gmail users in sophisticated phishing attacks
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson talks about a new AI-powered scam that targets Gmail users and is harder to detect unless you're very cautious.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside SSA’s plan to become the ‘most accessible agency’ in government
item.description: Betsy Beaumon, SSA’s chief transformation officer, says the is reinventing service delivery to keep up with a growing volume of customers. The post Inside SSA’s plan to become the ‘most accessible agency’ in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Mexico says foreign firms have pledged $20 billion in investments, but many are old or uncertain
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican officials announced Tuesday what they claimed was $20 billion in new foreign direct investment in Mexico, but much of that was neither new, nor completely certain.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Can Apple Watch 10 be used as a medical device?
item.description: It's increasingly clear that the future of the Apple Watch isn’t just as an alternative to cell phones.
item.dc:creator: David Lazarus, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft settles video gamers' lawsuit over Activision takeover
item.description: Microsoft Corp. has settled a lawsuit from a group of gamers who sued to try to stop the company from buying video game publisher Activision Blizzard for $69 billion last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The WordPress drama could have major implications for the internet
item.description: The WordPress situation has soured in the past several weeks, as a dispute spirals out of control between founder Matt Mullenweg and WP Engine, a platform that many users deploy in order to host their WordPress sites.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Hide and lock your apps on your iPhone with iOS 18
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explores the iOS 18’s new features to hide and lock apps for better privacy and organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At the mercy of social media
item.description: I like Instagram as much as the next guy. I check in daily on my carefully curated feed of folks I follow and then work hard not to doomscroll the day away. One of my favorite follows is an adorable Corgi (aren’t they all adorable?) named Parsnip.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How to manage generative AI programs – governance, education, regulation
item.description: Generative artificial intelligence has grown extremely rapidly, attracting investment and sparking developer interest and creativity. Companies see generative AI as a route to building innovative services and revolutionizing their industries.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Volunteers bring solar power to Hurricane Helene's disaster zone
item.description: BAKERSVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Nearly two weeks after Hurricane Helene downed power lines and washed out roads all over North Carolina's mountains, the constant din of a gas-powered generator is getting to be too much for Bobby Renfro.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA AOUN ANGUEIRA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Cisco revamps key DevNet sandboxes
item.description: Cisco has expanded its DevNet sandbox environments to make it easier for network professionals to test and develop applications for key enterprise networking technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World
